meta.incident: Connection slot exhaustion (FATAL: too many clients) combined with deadlock cascade on orders table, causing lock wait timeouts across 40+ backends; ultimately triggered server shutdown

ground_truth.needle: fatal:  sorry, too many clients already
ground_truth.desc: all 100/100 connection slots consumed, new connections rejected

ground_truth.needle: number of connection slots currently in use: 100/100
ground_truth.desc: connection pool hit the max_connections limit
circular lock dependency between backends on orders/inventory tables

ground_truth.needle: canceling statement due to lock timeout
ground_truth.desc: backends killed after waiting too long for locks
lock wait exceeded 1000 ms, deadlock imminent
postmaster shut down under connection pressure

context_loss: number of connection slots currently in use: 100/100

evidence.text: 2026-05-04 14:25:31.000 UTC [1600] app_user@appdb FATAL:  sorry, too many clients already
2026-05-04 14:25:32.023 UTC [1601] app_user@appdb FATAL:  sorry, too many clients already
2026-05-04 14:25:56.000 UTC [1550] app_user@appdb ERROR:  canceling statement due to lock timeout
2026-05-04 14:25:57.019 UTC [1551] app_user@appdb ERROR:  canceling statement due to lock timeout
2026-05-04 14:56:43.100 UTC [0] postgres FATAL:  pre-existing shared memory block is still in use

top_templates.pattern: UTC <*> app_user@appdb LOG: duration: <*> ms statement: <*> <*> <*> <*> <*> <*> WHERE <*> = $1
UTC <*> app_user@appdb LOG: duration: <*> ms statement: SELECT <*> FROM <*> WHERE <*> = <*> AND <*> > now() <*> <*> <*> <*>

UTC <*> app_user@appdb LOG: duration: <*> ms statement: SELECT p.*, c.name AS cat FROM products p JOIN categories c ON p.cat_id = c.id WHERE p.active = true AND c.visible = true ORDER BY p.created_at DESC LIMIT 50
UTC <*> app_user@appdb LOG: duration: <*> ms statement: SELECT u.id, u.email, count(o.id) AS order_count FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.user_id = u.id GROUP BY u.id HAVING count(o.id) > 10
UTC <*> app_user@appdb LOG: duration: <*> ms statement: INSERT INTO events (type, payload, created_at) SELECT 'order.created', row_to_json(o), now() FROM orders o WHERE o.id = $1
